metadata.name: AI Career Threat Index
metadata.creator: MeritForge AI
metadata.description: Open dataset scoring 76 professions on AI displacement risk (MIT licensed). Each role is decomposed into tasks, mapped against current AI capability and labor-market signals, and scored 0-100 (higher = greater displacement risk).
metadata.riskBandsNote: Scores within 5 points of a band boundary may retain their prior band across quarterly reviews for stability.

roles.title: Software Engineer
tasksAtRisk: Writing unit tests for simple functions
roles.insight: Software engineers who learn to direct AI coding tools are 2-3x more productive. The role is shifting from writing every line to orchestrating AI-generated code and owning architecture.

roles.title: Data Scientist
tasksAtRisk: Exploratory data analysis on structured datasets
tasksGrowing: Framing business problems as data problems
Designing novel ML approaches for unique challenges
roles.insight: AutoML handles routine modeling, but data scientists who can frame problems, design experiments, and translate findings into business action are more in demand than ever.

roles.title: DevOps Engineer
roles.insight: AI is automating routine DevOps tasks like pipeline configs and basic monitoring. Engineers who focus on platform strategy, security, and incident response will see their value grow.

roles.title: QA/Test Engineer
tasksAtRisk: Writing and running regression test suites
Bug report creation for common issues
tasksGrowing: Testing AI/ML system outputs for accuracy
User experience validation and edge cases
roles.insight: AI test generation tools can now write and maintain test suites faster than manual efforts. QA engineers should pivot toward AI output validation, security testing, and quality strategy.

roles.title: IT Support Specialist
tasksGrowing: AI tool administration and user training
roles.insight: AI chatbots and automated ticketing systems now resolve 40-60% of tier-1 support requests. IT specialists who move into security, cloud management, or AI tool administration will stay relevant.

roles.title: Web Developer
tasksGrowing: AI feature integration and prompt-driven UIs
roles.insight: AI can generate standard web pages and components quickly, but complex interactive applications, accessibility work, and AI-integrated features still need experienced developers.

roles.title: UX Designer
tasksGrowing: AI interaction design and conversational UX
Strategic design thinking for complex products
roles.insight: AI can generate mockups and variations fast, but understanding user needs, designing for AI-human interaction, and strategic design thinking remain deeply human skills.

roles.title: Database Administrator
tasksGrowing: Complex data architecture for AI workloads
roles.insight: Automated tuning and cloud-managed databases handle routine DBA tasks. Specialists in data architecture for AI workloads, vector databases, and security are increasingly sought after.

roles.title: Cybersecurity Analyst
tasksAtRisk: Log analysis for known threat patterns
tasksGrowing: Threat hunting for novel attack vectors
AI system security and adversarial defense
roles.insight: AI creates new attack surfaces faster than it automates defense. Security analysts who understand AI-specific threats — prompt injection, model poisoning, data exfiltration — are in high demand.

roles.title: Systems Administrator
roles.insight: Cloud platforms and AI-driven automation are replacing traditional sysadmin tasks. Moving toward cloud architecture, security, or platform engineering is the strongest career path forward.

roles.title: Financial Analyst
tasksGrowing: M&A due diligence and deal structuring
roles.insight: AI can build models and run analyses in minutes that used to take days. Analysts who focus on strategic interpretation, relationship management, and judgment calls under uncertainty will thrive.

roles.title: Accountant
roles.insight: Routine bookkeeping and tax prep are rapidly automating. Accountants who move into advisory, complex tax strategy, or forensic specialization will see growing demand.

roles.title: Management Consultant
tasksGrowing: Custom strategy for novel business challenges
roles.insight: AI handles research and deck-building faster, but clients pay for judgment, relationships, and implementation support. Consultants who specialize in AI transformation are in peak demand.

roles.title: Business Analyst
tasksGrowing: Complex process redesign with AI integration
roles.insight: AI can draft requirements and map simple processes, but understanding organizational politics, facilitating alignment across teams, and redesigning workflows around AI remain human strengths.

roles.title: Auditor
tasksGrowing: Risk assessment for complex, novel scenarios
AI system auditing and algorithmic accountability
roles.insight: AI can test 100% of transactions instead of samples, reshaping audit work. Auditors who specialize in AI systems auditing, IT risk, and fraud analytics will find strong career growth.

roles.title: Insurance Underwriter
tasksAtRisk: Standard risk assessment on common policies
tasksGrowing: Broker relationship management and deal structuring
roles.insight: AI now handles most standard personal lines underwriting. The career path forward is in complex commercial risks, emerging categories like cyber insurance, and relationship-driven deal structuring.

roles.title: Tax Preparer
roles.insight: AI tax software can now handle most individual returns with minimal human input. Tax professionals should specialize in complex situations — multi-state, international, or business tax planning.

roles.title: Loan Officer
tasksAtRisk: Rate comparison and loan product matching
tasksGrowing: Client relationship building and financial counseling
roles.insight: Online lenders and AI underwriting handle most standard loans now. Loan officers who build deep client relationships and master complex commercial lending remain valuable.

roles.title: Budget Analyst
tasksGrowing: AI-driven financial planning and scenario modeling
roles.insight: AI automates variance reporting and forecasting, but budget analysts who can advise leadership on resource strategy and facilitate cross-department negotiations add irreplaceable value.

roles.title: Compliance Officer
tasksGrowing: AI governance and ethical AI policy development
roles.insight: AI regulation is exploding — the EU AI Act, state-level laws, and industry standards all need human experts. Compliance officers who specialize in AI governance are entering a high-growth niche.

roles.title: Marketing Manager
tasksAtRisk: A/B test setup and basic analysis
tasksGrowing: Customer insight synthesis and persona development
roles.insight: AI handles campaign execution and reporting, but marketing managers who own brand strategy, customer insight, and cross-channel orchestration are more needed as channels multiply.

roles.title: Social Media Manager
tasksGrowing: Brand voice development and audience insight
roles.insight: AI can generate posts and schedule content, but authentic community engagement, crisis management, and brand voice consistency still depend on human judgment and emotional intelligence.

roles.title: SEO Specialist
roles.insight: AI tools automate keyword research and audits, and AI search is changing how people find content. SEO specialists who master AI search optimization and technical implementation will stay in demand.

roles.title: Copywriter
tasksAtRisk: Email subject lines and preview text
roles.insight: AI writes adequate copy fast and cheap. Copywriters who survive will be those who create brand voice, craft high-stakes persuasive copy, and develop creative campaigns AI cannot replicate.

roles.title: Content Writer
roles.insight: AI generates passable content at scale, crushing demand for generic writing. Content writers with deep subject expertise, original research ability, and editorial judgment will command premium rates.

roles.title: Sales Representative
tasksAtRisk: CRM data entry and pipeline updates
roles.insight: AI handles lead scoring and outreach automation, but complex B2B sales still depend on human trust, relationship-building, and the ability to understand nuanced client needs.

roles.title: Advertising Manager
roles.insight: AI optimizes bids and targeting automatically, but advertising managers who own creative strategy, manage privacy regulations, and orchestrate multi-channel campaigns remain essential.

roles.title: Market Research Analyst
roles.insight: AI processes survey data and tracks competitors faster than humans. Research analysts who excel at qualitative insight, asking the right questions, and translating data into strategy will thrive.

roles.title: PR Specialist
roles.insight: AI drafts releases and monitors coverage, but genuine media relationships, crisis management under pressure, and strategic narrative creation require human expertise and emotional intelligence.

roles.title: Email Marketing Specialist
tasksAtRisk: List segmentation based on standard criteria
roles.insight: AI generates subject lines, segments audiences, and even writes email copy. Specialists who focus on lifecycle strategy, deliverability expertise, and revenue attribution will stay ahead.

roles.title: Administrative Assistant

defenseSkills.skill: Executive Operations & Chief of Staff Skills

roles.insight: AI scheduling assistants, email drafters, and meeting summarizers are replacing core admin tasks. Pivoting toward project coordination, operations management, or executive chief-of-staff roles is key.

roles.title: Data Entry Clerk
tasksAtRisk: Manual data input from forms and documents
tasksGrowing: Data quality assurance and exception handling
roles.insight: OCR, AI document processing, and automated data extraction have made manual data entry largely obsolete. Building skills in data analytics or process automation is the strongest path forward.

roles.title: Executive Assistant
tasksGrowing: Strategic executive support and decision prep
roles.insight: AI handles scheduling and email, but executive assistants who function as strategic partners — managing confidential projects, preparing decision briefs, and handling stakeholder relationships — remain essential.

roles.title: Office Manager
tasksGrowing: Hybrid work policy and technology management
roles.insight: Automated procurement and digital workflows reduce traditional office manager tasks. Those who evolve into workplace experience leaders managing hybrid environments and team culture will grow.

roles.title: Receptionist
tasksAtRisk: Answering routine phone calls and routing
roles.insight: AI phone systems, digital check-in kiosks, and chatbot receptionists are widespread. Transitioning into facility management, security coordination, or customer experience roles offers more stability.

roles.title: Payroll Specialist
roles.insight: Payroll automation software handles most routine processing. Specialists who master complex multi-state compliance, system integrations, and compensation analytics will remain in demand.

roles.title: Procurement Specialist
tasksGrowing: Supply chain risk assessment and mitigation
roles.insight: AI automates purchase orders and vendor comparisons, but strategic sourcing, complex negotiations, and supply chain risk assessment need human judgment and relationship skills.

roles.title: Supply Chain Analyst
tasksGrowing: Sustainability and ESG supply chain planning
roles.insight: AI excels at demand forecasting and inventory optimization, but supply chain analysts who manage disruptions, design resilient networks, and plan for sustainability remain essential.

roles.title: Logistics Coordinator
roles.insight: AI handles routing, tracking, and rate comparison. Logistics coordinators who manage exceptions, build carrier relationships, and solve complex multi-modal transportation challenges add unique value.

roles.title: Facilities Manager
tasksGrowing: Workplace experience design for hybrid teams
Building technology integration and smart building management
roles.insight: AI sensors and smart building systems automate monitoring, but facilities managers who design workplace experiences, lead sustainability programs, and manage building technology portfolios are growing in importance.

roles.title: Physician
tasksAtRisk: Reviewing standard lab results and imaging
tasksGrowing: Complex diagnostic reasoning and differential diagnosis
roles.insight: AI assists with documentation, diagnostics, and drug checks, but physicians who combine clinical judgment with AI tools become more effective — not less needed. The human element of medicine is irreplaceable.

roles.title: Nurse (RN)
tasksGrowing: Direct patient care and clinical assessment
roles.insight: AI reduces documentation burden and flags potential issues, freeing nurses for what matters most — direct patient care. Nursing demand continues to outpace supply significantly.

roles.title: Pharmacist
roles.insight: Automated dispensing and AI interaction checkers handle routine pharmacy tasks. Pharmacists expanding into clinical consultation, medication therapy management, and personalized medicine will flourish.

roles.title: Medical Coder
tasksAtRisk: Assigning ICD-10 and CPT codes from notes
tasksGrowing: AI coding tool oversight and quality assurance
roles.insight: AI coding engines now assign codes with 90%+ accuracy for common encounters. Coders who specialize in complex cases, audit AI-generated codes, and lead documentation improvement programs will stay relevant.

roles.title: Radiologist
tasksAtRisk: Screening standard imaging for common findings
Measurements and quantification on routine scans
tasksGrowing: AI model validation and quality oversight
roles.insight: AI flags findings and triages urgent cases, but radiologists who interpret complex ambiguous images, perform interventional procedures, and validate AI outputs remain essential to patient safety.

roles.title: Healthcare Administrator
tasksGrowing: Healthcare strategy and service line development
Patient experience and quality improvement leadership
roles.insight: AI optimizes scheduling, billing, and reporting, but healthcare administrators who lead AI implementation, manage regulatory complexity, and improve patient experience are growing in strategic importance.

roles.title: Physical Therapist
roles.insight: Physical therapy is inherently hands-on and relationship-based. AI handles documentation and tracking, actually freeing PTs to focus on what they do best — treating patients. Demand is strong and growing.

roles.title: Medical Transcriptionist
roles.insight: AI speech-to-text has largely replaced manual medical transcription. Transitioning into clinical documentation improvement, health informatics, or medical coding offers a stronger career trajectory.

roles.title: Lab Technician
roles.insight: Automated analyzers and AI handle routine testing, but lab techs who manage automation systems, troubleshoot complex specimens, and validate new testing methods remain essential to clinical labs.

roles.title: Mental Health Counselor
roles.insight: Mental health care is fundamentally about human connection. AI chatbots handle surface-level check-ins, but therapy requires empathy, clinical judgment, and trust that AI cannot replicate. Demand is surging.

roles.title: Lawyer
tasksAtRisk: Legal research and case law review
Brief section drafting for routine motions
tasksGrowing: Complex deal structuring and novel legal questions

defenseSkills.skill: AI Legal Research Tools (Westlaw AI, CoCounsel)

roles.insight: AI accelerates research and drafting, making lawyers more productive — not obsolete. Lawyers who master AI tools and specialize in AI-related law (IP, liability, regulation) are positioned for growth.

roles.title: Paralegal
tasksGrowing: Complex litigation support and case strategy
roles.insight: AI handles document review and standard research at scale. Paralegals who master legal AI tools, specialize in complex practice areas, and manage technology-driven workflows will remain valuable.

roles.title: Legal Secretary
tasksAtRisk: Calendar management and court deadline tracking
roles.insight: AI handles document formatting, scheduling, and correspondence. Legal secretaries should consider paralegal certification and legal technology specialization to expand their role and career options.

roles.title: Contract Reviewer
tasksAtRisk: Risk scoring on standard contract types
tasksGrowing: AI contract tool training and oversight
roles.insight: AI contract analysis tools review thousands of contracts in hours. Reviewers who oversee AI output, handle complex non-standard agreements, and inform negotiation strategy will continue to add value.

roles.title: Compliance Analyst
tasksGrowing: AI regulatory compliance (EU AI Act, state laws)
roles.insight: AI tracks regulatory changes and generates reports, but compliance analysts who specialize in AI-specific regulations and complex cross-border requirements are entering a high-growth niche.

roles.title: Teacher (K-12)
tasksAtRisk: Lesson plan drafting and resource gathering
roles.insight: AI generates lesson plans and grades quizzes, freeing teachers for what matters most — mentoring students, managing classrooms, and providing the human connection young learners need.

roles.title: College Professor
roles.insight: AI assists with course materials and grading, but professors who produce original research, facilitate deep learning through discussion, and mentor the next generation are irreplaceable in higher education.

roles.title: Corporate Trainer
tasksAtRisk: Creating training slide decks and handouts
roles.insight: AI creates training materials and tracks progress, but live facilitation, organizational change leadership, and designing AI upskilling programs are high-value human contributions.

roles.title: Instructional Designer
roles.insight: AI generates course content and assessments quickly, but instructional designers who apply learning science, design immersive experiences, and align training with business outcomes will stay in demand.

roles.title: Academic Advisor
roles.insight: AI chatbots answer routine questions and check degree requirements. Advisors who focus on student success coaching, career exploration, and at-risk intervention provide the human support students need most.

roles.title: Tutor
tasksGrowing: Executive function and study skills coaching
roles.insight: AI tutors like Khanmigo explain concepts 24/7, but human tutors who provide accountability, adapt to emotional needs, and coach executive function skills offer something AI cannot match.

roles.title: Graphic Designer
tasksAtRisk: Creating social media graphics from templates

defenseSkills.skill: AI Design Tools (Midjourney, Firefly, Figma AI)

roles.insight: AI generates graphics and variations in seconds, reducing demand for production design. Graphic designers who own brand systems, creative direction, and motion design will remain highly valued.

roles.title: Video Editor
tasksAtRisk: Rough cut assembly from raw footage
roles.insight: AI handles rough cuts, captions, and color grading, but skilled editors who craft compelling narratives, manage complex projects, and collaborate with clients will continue to command premium rates.

roles.title: Photographer
tasksAtRisk: Basic photo editing and batch processing
tasksGrowing: Creative direction and art direction for shoots
roles.insight: AI generates stock imagery and handles editing, but photographers who show up in person — for events, portraits, and specialized shoots — provide an experience AI cannot replicate.

roles.title: Technical Writer
tasksAtRisk: User guide drafts from feature specs
tasksGrowing: Technical content for marketing and thought leadership
roles.insight: AI generates documentation from code effectively, but technical writers who design information architecture, improve developer experience, and create strategic content are becoming more valuable.

roles.title: Journalist
tasksAtRisk: Earnings report summaries and data-driven stories
tasksGrowing: Analysis and opinion with unique perspective
roles.insight: AI writes commodity news (earnings, scores, weather), but journalists who do original reporting, investigate stories, and bring unique perspective are more important in an AI-saturated information environment.

roles.title: Music Producer
tasksAtRisk: Creating beats and loops from samples
tasksGrowing: Complex album production and sonic identity
roles.insight: AI generates beats and background music, but producers who shape an artist's sonic vision, engineer live performances, and craft full albums bring creative judgment AI lacks.

roles.title: HR Manager
tasksGrowing: AI policy development and workforce planning
roles.insight: AI automates policy lookups and benefits processing, but HR managers who build culture, resolve complex employee situations, and develop AI workforce strategies are more important than ever.

roles.title: Recruiter
tasksGrowing: Complex hiring strategy for leadership roles
Employer branding and talent market intelligence
roles.insight: AI screens resumes and writes outreach at scale, but recruiters who build genuine candidate relationships, sell opportunities to passive talent, and advise on hiring strategy add irreplaceable value.

roles.title: Compensation Analyst
tasksAtRisk: Market salary data collection and benchmarking
roles.insight: AI automates benchmarking and analysis, but compensation analysts who design total rewards strategy, handle executive comp, and ensure pay transparency compliance are in growing demand.

roles.title: Training Coordinator
tasksAtRisk: Training session scheduling and room booking
tasksGrowing: Learning program strategy and ROI measurement
AI upskilling program design and delivery
roles.insight: AI handles scheduling, tracking, and survey distribution, but training coordinators who design AI upskilling programs and measure learning ROI position themselves as strategic partners.

roles.title: HR Generalist
tasksAtRisk: Employee FAQ responses and policy lookups
Leave tracking and basic case management
tasksGrowing: HR technology implementation and change management
roles.insight: AI chatbots answer routine HR questions and manage onboarding checklists. Generalists who focus on employee relations, experience design, and HR technology management will see their roles expand.

roles.title: Project Manager
tasksAtRisk: Meeting notes and action item tracking

defenseSkills.skill: AI Project Management Tools (Notion AI, Asana AI)

roles.insight: AI automates status reports, scheduling, and action tracking. Project managers who lead stakeholder alignment, manage risks, and motivate teams through complex programs are more needed, not less.

roles.title: Product Manager
roles.insight: AI gathers data and drafts specs, but product managers who define product vision, make hard prioritization calls, and align organizations around a strategy are among the most AI-resilient roles.

roles.title: Scrum Master
tasksGrowing: Team coaching and psychological safety building
roles.insight: AI tracks metrics and generates reports, but Scrum Masters who build psychological safety, coach teams through conflict, and remove organizational impediments bring value that requires human skill.

roles.title: Program Manager
roles.insight: AI aggregates status and tracks dependencies, but program managers who align multiple programs to business strategy, manage executive relationships, and mitigate complex risks are deeply valued.
